maxContractTerm.value: No statutory maximum found in the West Virginia Code. The county-board transportation authority (W. Va. Code §18-5-13) is silent as to contract term length for student-transportation services contracts with private vendors. The binding state-board legislative rule — Policy 8200 (W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202), adopted under W. Va. Code §§18-2-5 and 18-5-13 — provides at §3.2.5.b (Life of Contract): 'Contracts normally cover a 12-month period or cite a specific time for completion for the project or service. A solicitation for a contract that includes an option on the part of the LEA to extend or renew the contract for an additional period may be advantageous and may be considered.' Combined with §3.2.5.c's hard cap that renewals 'are limited to two successive one year periods,' the effective administrative ceiling on a standard 12-month award plus permitted renewals is approximately three years total, absent a longer originally bid/solicited term and absent a stricter local LEA policy.
maxContractTerm.citation: W. Va. Code §18-5-13 (silent as to term) — https://code.wvlegislature.gov/18-5-13/ ; W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 (WV Board of Education Policy 8200, Purchasing Policies and Procedures Manual for LEAs), §§3.2.5.b (Life of Contract) and 3.2.5.c (Renewals) — https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf (WVDE searchable copy of 126 CSR 202)

rebidRequired.label: Conditional
rebidRequired.rule: No transportation-specific rebid statute exists in the West Virginia Code. Under the generally applicable WV Board of Education legislative rule (Policy 8200, 126 CSR 202), a county board (LEA) must use competitive bidding to purchase services — including student-transportation services — once the estimated cost reaches the §7.11 thresholds: <$5,000 (competitive bids encouraged but not required); $5,000 to <$10,000 (three verbal quotes required, where practical); $10,000 to <$25,000 (three written bids required, where practical); $25,000 to <$50,000 (advertised bids required); $50,000 or more (publicly advertised sealed bids required). Limited exemptions/alternatives apply (sole source / direct purchase under §3.2.5.a(6); piggybacking on a state open-ended contract under §3.2.5.a.c and §8.1.4; cooperative/RESA purchasing under §§8.1.7–8.1.8; and statutorily exempt services under §9). After a contract's awarded term (typically 12 months) expires and any permitted renewals are exhausted ('limited to two successive one year periods,' §3.2.5.c), the LEA must re-solicit competitive bids under the same threshold framework to award a new contract. The county board has no rule-based authority to renew a transportation services contract beyond two successive one-year renewals without re-bidding.
rebidRequired.citation: W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 (Policy 8200), §§3.2.5, 7.11.1–7.11.5, 8.1, and 9 (Exemptions) — https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf ; see also W. Va. Code §5A-3-11 (state contracts for school buses — equipment, not services) — https://code.wvlegislature.gov/5A-3-11/

renewalMechanics.value: Renewal without a new competitive solicitation is permitted only on the terms set out in Policy 8200 §3.2.5.c: a contract 'extends for a period of one year or until such "reasonable time" thereafter as is necessary to obtain a new contract or renew the original contract. The "reasonable time" period shall not exceed twelve months. ... Contracts that contain renewal provisions may be renewed upon the mutual written consent of the LEA and vendor prior to the expiration date. A letter of justification should be maintained on file, stating pertinent reasons for the recommendation to renew an existing contract. These factors include, but are not limited to, vendor performance, market conditions and other analytical measures that indicate that renewing the contract is in the best interest of the LEA. Renewals shall be in accordance with the terms and conditions of the original contract and are limited to two successive one year periods.' Renewal therefore requires (1) an express renewal option in the original solicitation/contract, (2) mutual written consent of the LEA and vendor before expiration, (3) a written letter of justification on file, and (4) compliance with the two-successive-one-year cap. Price adjustments during the contract period are allowed only if 'the conditions under which they are authorized must be specified in the original solicitation and resulting contract' (§3.2.5.b). The West Virginia Code and Policy 8200 do not provide for automatic/evergreen rollover, statutory CPI escalators, statutory fuel-adjustment formulas, or a state-agency renewal-approval requirement; all renewal mechanics flow from the original solicitation document and the standard terms in Policy 8200.
renewalMechanics.citation: W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 (Policy 8200), §§3.2.5.b (Life of Contract) and 3.2.5.c (Renewals) — https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf

finalFields.statutoryCitation: Primary statute: W. Va. Code §18-5-13 (Authority of boards generally — county-board transportation, school-bus operation and leasing, and the insurance requirement for 'Any contractual agreement for transportation of students') — https://code.wvlegislature.gov/18-5-13/ . Operative legislative rule: W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 — WV Board of Education Policy 8200, 'Purchasing Policies and Procedures Manual for Local Educational Agencies' (filed July 13, 2012; effective August 13, 2012), particularly §§3.2.5.b (Life of Contract), 3.2.5.c (Renewals), 7.11 (Competitive Bid Threshold Limits), 8 (Alternative Procurement Procedures), and 9 (Exemptions). Statutory authority recited for Policy 8200 includes W. Va. Code §§18-2-5, 18-5-5, 18-5-13, 5A-3-37, 5A-3-37a, and 18-9B-12 — https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf . Related transportation-funding section: W. Va. Code §18-9A-7 (foundation allowance recognizing 'contracted transportation services') — https://code.wvlegislature.gov/18-9A-7/ . Related school-bus regulation: W. Va. C.S.R. §126-92 (Policy 4336, School Bus Transportation Regulations) and W. Va. Code §17C-14-12 (school-bus rules adopted by reference into county contracts) — https://code.wvlegislature.gov/17C-14-12/ — neither sets contract term, renewal, or procurement rules.

districtVariance.label: Unknown
districtVariance.explanation: Policy 8200 (126 CSR 202) describes itself as establishing the minimum statewide system of LEA purchasing rules and expressly contemplates that each LEA will 'develop local policies, practices and procedures that supplement the provisions specified herein,' which 'may make the requirements more restrictive.' Structurally, county boards may adopt local purchasing policies that lower bidding thresholds or shorten renewal periods, but they cannot relax the Policy 8200 floor — they cannot, for example, extend renewals beyond two successive one-year periods. No statewide West Virginia Department of Education bulletin, state-auditor report, AG opinion, or West Virginia School Boards Association guidance documenting either uniform application or actual variance in district interpretation of these specific transportation-contract provisions was located in the time-bounded search; nor did a targeted check of large and small/rural district policy/purchasing portals (Kanawha, Berkeley, Cabell-region) surface a published district policy that materially diverges from Policy 8200 in a load-bearing way for the five fields. The structural permission for stricter local supplementation means actual district practice likely varies in detail, but variance in interpretation of the underlying state-board rule is not affirmatively established by located sources.

specialEducation.detail: W. Va. Code §18-20-2(a) lists 'transportation' as a special service the county board 'shall provide' to implement each student's IEP, and §18-20-2(b) authorizes a county board to 'provide for educating resident exceptional children by contracting with other counties or other educational agencies which maintain special education facilities' — an inter-LEA contracting authority, not a private-vendor procurement rule. Neither §18-20-2, nor W. Va. C.S.R. §126-92 (Policy 4336, including §17.5 governing 'contract operators' in limited-service areas, which sets background-check, training, and $1,000,000 auto-liability requirements but no term/renewal/procurement rule), nor W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 (Policy 8200) establishes a different maximum contract term, different rebidding standard, or different renewal mechanism for special-education, IEP-mandated, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, alternative, or disability-student transportation than for regular-route transportation. Policy 4373 (LEA student conduct/safety) and Policy 2419 / 126 CSR 16 (Regulations for the Education of Students with Exceptionalities) address bus modifications and supports specified in IEPs and §504 plans, but they go to the duty to transport and operational accommodations rather than to contract term, renewal, or procurement. Federal IDEA obligations attach to the duty to transport but do not displace West Virginia's state contract and procurement rules.

perFieldVerdicts.note: W. Va. Code §18-5-13 re-opened at code.wvlegislature.gov — confirmed silent on contract term length for student-transportation services contracts. Policy 8200 §3.2.5.b ('Life of Contract — Contracts normally cover a 12-month period or cite a specific time for completion for the project or service…') and §3.2.5.c ('Renewals shall be in accordance with the terms and conditions of the original contract and are limited to two successive one year periods') verified verbatim against the WVDE searchable PDF. No transportation-specific code section setting a contract term limit was located in W. Va. Code Chapter 18, Article 5; W. Va. Code §17C-14-12 incorporates BOE design/operation rules into contracts but sets no term limit; W. Va. Code §5A-3-11 covers state contracts for school-bus equipment purchase, not transportation services.

Policy 8200 §7.11 thresholds verified verbatim in the WVDE PDF table of contents and the §7.11.1–7.11.5 narrative (<$5,000 encouraged; $5,000–<$10,000 verbal quotes; $10,000–<$25,000 written bids; $25,000–<$50,000 advertised bids; ≥$50,000 sealed advertised bids). Exemptions (sole source / direct purchase, piggybacking on state open-ended contract, RESA cooperative purchasing, AEPA/U.S. Communities/TCPN national cooperatives, sole-source waiver of advertising) verified in §§3.2.5.a, 8.1, and 9 of the same document. No transportation-specific carve-out from competitive bidding was located. W. Va. Code §5-22-1 confirmed applicable only to construction projects, not transportation services. W. Va. Code §5A-3-10 confirmed applicable only to state spending units, not county boards of education.

Policy 8200 §3.2.5.c renewal language verified verbatim ('mutual written consent of the LEA and vendor prior to the expiration date'; 'letter of justification should be maintained on file'; 'Renewals shall be in accordance with the terms and conditions of the original contract and are limited to two successive one year periods'). The 'reasonable time' clause limiting bridging periods to twelve months was likewise verified. No statutory CPI, fuel-escalator, automatic rollover, evergreen-renewal, or state-agency-approval mechanism for renewing student-transportation contracts was located in either the West Virginia Code or in Policy 8200.

Primary statute (W. Va. Code §18-5-13) and operative legislative rule (W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 / Policy 8200) re-opened and confirmed at the official sources. Adjacent citations also re-verified: W. Va. Code §18-20-2 (special education); §18-9A-7 (foundation funding allowance, including 'contracted transportation services'); §5-22-1 (construction-only competitive bidding); §5A-3-10 (state-spending-unit purchasing); §5A-3-11 (state contracts on school-bus equipment); §17C-14-12 (BOE school-bus rules adopted by reference into county contracts); and W. Va. C.S.R. §126-92 (Policy 4336).

Policy 8200's express minimum-floor / 'may make the requirements more restrictive' design re-confirmed. Targeted searches for documented variance in interpretation across districts surfaced general county-purchasing portals (e.g., Kanawha-county purchasing portal) but no document affirmatively establishing either uniform application or actual interpretive variance of the five-field framework for student-transportation contracts. The 'Unknown' label is preserved as a documented negative result, not as inference.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Max Contract Term — no statutory maximum; effective administrative ceiling is original term (typically 12 months) plus up to two one-year renewals
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A West Virginia statute, administrative rule, AG opinion, or BOE policy authorizes a longer single award term (e.g., 'multi-year', 'five-year', 'option', 'successive', 'extension', 'not exceed') or carves out student-transportation contracts from the Policy 8200 renewal cap.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-fetched W. Va. Code §18-5-13 and confirmed silence on term. Searched W. Va. Code Chapter 18 Article 5 section list; Chapter 5A Article 3 (Purchasing Division); Chapter 5-22 (construction-only competitive bidding); Chapter 17C-14 (school bus rules). Search strings: 'West Virginia school bus contract term limit'; 'West Virginia transportation contract multi-year'; 'West Virginia code transportation contract "not exceed"'; 'West Virginia code transportation contract "five-year" OR "5-year"'. Checked legislature.gov 2025–2026 bill text database.
challengeMatrix.result: No longer-term or carve-out authority located. Policy 8200 §3.2.5.b and §3.2.5.c verified verbatim in the WVDE searchable PDF.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains Stage 1 finding; confidence remains Medium because the load-bearing rule is in 126 CSR 202, not a transportation-specific codified statute.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Rebid Required at Term-End — 'Conditional': competitive process required at expiration once thresholds met, with exemptions for sole source, piggyback, RESA/national cooperative, and statutorily exempt services
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: An exemption (board waiver, emergency procurement, negotiated procurement, state-agency renewal approval, small-purchase threshold, or transportation-specific carve-out) allows the LEA to renew or extend the contract beyond two one-year renewals without competitive bidding, OR a mandatory bidding statute supersedes Policy 8200's threshold framework.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-read Policy 8200 §§3.2.5 (Selection of Procurement Method), 8 (Alternative Procurement Procedures), 9 (Exemptions). Verified §5-22-1 (construction only, doesn't reach service contracts), §5A-3-10 (state spending units, doesn't reach county BOE service contracts), §5A-3-11 (state contracts for school bus equipment, not services). Search strings included 'sole source', 'piggyback', 'cooperative', 'emergency procurement', 'small purchase threshold' against the WVDE PDF text.
challengeMatrix.result: Exemptions are real and limited; none reach to extend a transportation services contract past the two-renewal cap or to convert renewal into open-ended rollover. Mandatory-bidding statutes (§5-22-1, §5A-3-10) do not displace Policy 8200's thresholds for county-board service contracts.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Renewal Mechanics — mutual written consent + letter of justification + two-successive-one-year cap; no CPI/fuel/evergreen/state-approval mechanism
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A statutory or regulatory provision authorizes CPI escalation, fuel-cost pass-through, automatic/evergreen rollover, board-waiver renewal, or a state-agency-approval pathway for renewing transportation contracts beyond the two-renewal cap.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Verified §3.2.5.b and §3.2.5.c verbatim in the WVDE PDF — language is exhaustive and limits price adjustments to those 'specified in the original solicitation and resulting contract'. Searched W. Va. Code Chapter 18, 18A, and 5A for transportation-specific renewal authority. Searched 126 CSR 92 (Policy 4336) for any contract-procurement language: only §17.5 (contract operators in limited-service areas) located, with no term/renewal/procurement provisions.
challengeMatrix.result: No CPI/fuel/evergreen/state-approval renewal mechanism located. Renewal mechanics are exhausted by §3.2.5.c and the originating solicitation/contract.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Statutory Citation — W. Va. Code §18-5-13 plus W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 (Policy 8200) is the operative authority
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A different transportation-specific code section, AG opinion, or BOE policy actually controls contract term, rebid, and renewal for student-transportation services.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened all cited official URLs. Cross-checked W. Va. Code §17C-14-12 (school bus rules), §18-2E-5d (transportation time recommendations — not contract rules), §18-5G-5 (charter schools — silent on transportation contracting), §18-9A-7 (funding — silent on procurement), §18-20-2 (special ed — silent on procurement). Searched for transportation-specific procurement carve-outs in Chapter 18 Article 5 section list.
challengeMatrix.result: No alternative controlling authority located. Combined §18-5-13 + Policy 8200 framework confirmed as operative.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Special-Education Differentiation — same rules as regular-route transportation
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A separate statute, BOE policy, or regulation establishes different contract-term, renewal, or procurement rules for IEP, McKinney-Vento, foster-care, homeless-student, alternative, or disability transportation.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Re-opened §18-20-2 and confirmed only inter-LEA contracting authority, no private-vendor procurement carve-out. Re-checked W. Va. C.S.R. §126-92-17.5 (contract operators) and confirmed it sets only individual-operator qualifications. Searched 126 CSR 16 (Policy 2419, Regulations for the Education of Students with Exceptionalities) and confirmed it addresses IEP-based bus modifications and supports rather than contract term/renewal/procurement. Searched 'McKinney-Vento West Virginia transportation contract procurement' — no separate procurement rule located.
challengeMatrix.result: No separate special-education procurement rule located. Same Policy 8200 framework applies.

challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: Affirmative evidence exists of materially divergent district interpretation of these five-field Policy 8200 provisions (a state-auditor finding, an AG opinion, a WVDE bulletin, or two district policies that obviously diverge from Policy 8200's floor in a load-bearing way).
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Targeted searches against 'Kanawha purchasing policy transportation', 'Berkeley purchasing transportation contract', 'Cabell schools purchasing transportation contract'; reviewed Kanawha County purchasing portal; searched WVDE bulletins for transportation-contract variance findings; searched WV State Auditor reports for procurement audit findings on transportation contracts.
challengeMatrix.result: No affirmative documentation of either uniform application or material interpretive variance for the five-field framework. Policy 8200 structurally invites stricter local supplementation but the empirical variance question remains a documented negative.
challengeMatrix.confidenceImpact: Sustains 'Unknown' label as documented negative result.

challengeMatrix.findingChallenged: Currentness — Policy 8200 / 126 CSR 202 (eff. Aug 13, 2012) remains operative; no post-2024 amendment supersedes Stage 1 findings
challengeMatrix.disconfirmingHypothesis: A 2024–2026 W. Va. Legislature enrolled bill, BOE policy amendment, or session-law amendment to §18-5-13, §18-20-2, or 126 CSR 202 changes the contract-term, rebid, or renewal framework for student-transportation services.
challengeMatrix.challengeSearches: Searched WV Legislature 2026 RS bill list and 2025 RS bill list for amendments touching §18-5-13, §18-20-2, school-bus contracting, pupil-transportation procurement, county-board purchasing, or Policy 8200 / 126 CSR 202. Located SB 428 (2026 RS — bus operator job titles/pay), HB 4940/4942 (2026 RS — bus operations) — none of which change the term/rebid/renewal framework.

sourcesReverified: WV BOE Policy 8200 (W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202), §§3.2.5.b, 3.2.5.c, 7.11, 8.1, 9, 19.1 — https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf (WVDE searchable copy)

W. Va. C.S.R. §126-92-17.5 (Policy 4336 contract operators) — https://www.law.cornell.edu/regulations/west-virginia/W-Va-C-S-R-SS-126-92-17 (Cornell LII pathfinder); official version — https://apps.sos.wv.gov/adlaw/csr/readfile.aspx?DocId=52989&Format=PDF
sourcesUnreachable: WV SOS official PDF of 126 CSR 202 — https://apps.sos.wv.gov/adlaw/csr/readfile.aspx?DocId=23899&Format=PDF — the official SOS PDF is a scanned/image-only document (verified again here) and is not text-extractable; the WVDE-distributed searchable copy at https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf was used as the operative text, consistent with WVDE's current 'Manuals and Policies' page that links it as the searchable copy.

sourceLedger.citation: W. Va. Code §18-5-13 (Authority of boards generally)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Official code site does not display a 'current through' banner; the site states sections are 'updated regularly as new legislation takes effect.' Section read directly from code.wvlegislature.gov on 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 2, 3, 4 (transportation authority; statute silent on max term, rebid, and renewal); special-education differentiation
sourceLedger.relevantText: Authorizes county boards to 'Provide transportation according to rules established by the county board'; subsection authorizes lease of school buses; subsection requires that 'Any contractual agreement for transportation of students shall require the vehicle owner to maintain insurance coverage against negligence in an amount specified by the county board.' Statute is silent on max contract term, rebid requirement, and renewal mechanics for student-transportation services contracts.

sourceLedger.citation: W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 — WV Board of Education Policy 8200, Purchasing Policies and Procedures Manual for Local Educational Agencies, §§3.2.5.b, 3.2.5.c, 7.11.1–7.11.5, 8.1, 9, and 19.1.1
sourceLedger.officialUrl: https://wvde.us/sites/default/files/2017/10/Policy8200_with_Cover.pdf (WVDE-distributed searchable copy; the SOS official PDF at https://apps.sos.wv.gov/adlaw/csr/readfile.aspx?DocId=23899&Format=PDF is scanned image-only)
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Filed July 13, 2012; effective August 13, 2012; no superseding amendment located through 2026-06-24.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Fields 1, 2, 3, 5 (Life of Contract default; renewal cap; competitive bid thresholds; minimum-floor / structurally permissive of stricter local LEA policy)
sourceLedger.relevantText: §3.2.5.b 'Life of Contract — Contracts normally cover a 12-month period or cite a specific time for completion for the project or service. A solicitation for a contract that includes an option on the part of the LEA to extend or renew the contract for an additional period may be advantageous and may be considered.' §3.2.5.c 'Renewals: ... Contracts that contain renewal provisions may be renewed upon the mutual written consent of the LEA and vendor prior to the expiration date. ... Renewals shall be in accordance with the terms and conditions of the original contract and are limited to two successive one year periods.' §7.11 thresholds: <$5,000 encouraged; $5,000–<$10,000 three verbal quotes; $10,000–<$25,000 three written bids; $25,000–<$50,000 advertised bids; ≥$50,000 publicly advertised sealed bids. §8.1 alternative procurement (no bidder response; sole source; statewide piggyback; GSA schedule; RESA cooperative; AEPA/U.S. Communities/TCPN). §9 exemptions from competitive bid (accounting/audits and other listed services). §19.1.1 confirms a separate framework for school bus equipment under W. Va. Code §5A-3-11.

sourceLedger.citation: W. Va. C.S.R. §126-92 — WV Board of Education Policy 4336, School Bus Transportation Regulations, §17 (Operator Candidates and Contract Operators), §17.5
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Most recently amended Feb. 2020 per WVDE-distributed version.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation; confirms 126 CSR 92 imposes only operator-qualification and safety rules — not contract term, renewal, or procurement
sourceLedger.relevantText: §17.5 authorizes 'Counties may elect to hire a contract operator to transport students in passenger vehicles with a designed seating capacity of fewer than ten occupants, not including operator, in areas where bus service is limited due to terrain' and prescribes CIB/FBI/DMV background checks, ≥8 hours of training, vehicle inspections/maintenance, and a $1,000,000 per-occurrence auto-liability minimum. No language on contract term length, contract duration, renewal, or procurement.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Special-education differentiation (no separate procurement, term, or rebid rule)
sourceLedger.relevantText: (a) Each county board shall provide 'special services' including 'transportation, and related services' to implement each student's IEP. (b) 'A county board may provide for educating resident exceptional children by contracting with other counties or other educational agencies which maintain special education facilities.' 'Fiscal matters shall follow policies approved by the state board.' No provision establishes a different contract term, renewal mechanism, rebid requirement, or procurement procedure for special-education transportation.

sourceLedger.citation: W. Va. Code §18-9A-7 (Foundation allowance for transportation)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Supports that 'contracted transportation services' is a recognized funding category but contains no procurement, term, or rebid rule
sourceLedger.relevantText: Funding formula references 'transportation costs incurred by the county for maintenance, operation and related costs ... including the costs incurred for contracted transportation services'. No procurement, term, renewal, or rebid provision.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Negative result — confirms general construction-bidding statute applies to county boards of education for construction projects only, not transportation services contracts
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'The state and its subdivisions shall, except as provided in this section, solicit competitive bids for every construction project exceeding $50,000 in total cost.' Definition of 'state and/or its subdivisions' explicitly includes 'all county boards of education,' but the statute's scope is limited to 'construction project' work (building, erecting, constructing, adding, repairing, remodeling, etc.). Transportation services are not a 'construction project' under this definition.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Negative result — applies to state 'spending units,' not directly to county boards of education
sourceLedger.relevantText: Governs procurement by state spending units through the Purchasing Division; does not set a maximum service-contract term or rebidding rule applicable to county boards' transportation contracts. Time-specific provisions address only continuing procurements over $1 million by state spending units (12-month advance notice; 180-day award/termination after specs approved).

sourceLedger.citation: W. Va. Code §5A-3-11 (State contracts for school buses)
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 2 — confirms separate framework for state bids on school-bus equipment purchase (not service contracts)
sourceLedger.relevantText: 'state bids on school buses shall be accepted from all bidders who shall then be awarded contracts if they meet the state board's Minimum Standards for Design and Equipment of School Buses. County boards of education may select from those bidders who have been awarded contracts and shall pay the difference between the state aid formula amount and the actual cost of bus replacement.' Statute reaches bus equipment purchase, not private transportation services contracts.

sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Field 4 — confirms BOE design/operation rules are incorporated by reference into county-board transportation contracts but no term/renewal/procurement rule
sourceLedger.relevantText: WV BOE 'by and with the advice of the motor vehicle commissioner shall adopt and enforce rules ... to govern the design and operation of all school buses'; these rules 'shall by reference be made a part of any such contract with a county board of education'; non-compliance is 'breach of contract' and the contract 'shall be canceled after notice and hearing.' Statute is silent on contract term length, renewal, and procurement procedure.

sourceLedger.sourceTier: 4 - Official agency guidance citing binding authority
sourceLedger.codeCurrency: Hosted page references the current operative version of Policy 8200; underlying legislative rule is 126 CSR 202 (eff. 2012-08-13). Page last updated May 5, 2026.
sourceLedger.fieldsSupported: Confirms WVDE-published, currently-operative Policy 8200 is the binding state-board legislative rule governing county-board purchasing
sourceLedger.relevantText: WVDE distributes both the 'Official Copy' (scanned image PDF) and the 'Searchable Copy' of Policy 8200 — the 'Purchasing Policies and Procedures Manual for Local Educational Agencies' that implements 126 CSR 202.

verificationNotes.searchStrategy: Stage 2 re-opened every Stage-1 citation at its official URL (or, where the SOS PDF was scanned image-only, at the WVDE-distributed searchable copy of the same legislative rule) and verified each quoted phrase verbatim against the document text. Then ran disconfirming searches against the West Virginia Code, the West Virginia Code of State Rules, the WV Legislature 2025 and 2026 Regular Session bill databases, and WVDE/AG/Auditor guidance, looking for: a longer single-award authority, a transportation-specific carve-out from Policy 8200's renewal cap, a special-education procurement rule, a state-agency renewal-approval pathway, a CPI/fuel/evergreen mechanism, and documented district interpretive variance. Search strings included 'school bus contract term limit'; 'pupil transportation contract renewal'; '"not exceed" school bus West Virginia'; 'transportation contract piggyback West Virginia'; 'special education transportation contract procurement West Virginia'; 'West Virginia code §18-5-13 amended'; '2026 SB school bus transportation contract'.
verificationNotes.currentLawCheck: WV Code: code.wvlegislature.gov does not display a 'current through' banner — the site states sections are 'updated regularly as new legislation takes effect.' All cited code sections (§18-5-13, §18-20-2, §18-9A-7, §5-22-1, §5A-3-10, §5A-3-11, §17C-14-12, §18-5G-5) read directly from code.wvlegislature.gov on 2026-06-24. Policy 8200 / 126 CSR 202: filed July 13, 2012; effective August 13, 2012; the WVDE 'Manuals and Policies' page (last updated May 5, 2026) continues to publish the same operative version of Policy 8200; no superseding amendment was located. Policy 4336 / 126 CSR 92: most recently amended Feb. 2020; section 17.5 (contract operators) re-verified. 2025 and 2026 Regular Session bill lists were searched for amendments touching §18-5-13, §18-20-2, school-bus contracting, pupil-transportation procurement, county-board purchasing, or Policy 8200 / 126 CSR 202; no superseding enacted bill was located.
verificationNotes.unresolvedIssues: Confidence finalized at Medium (not High) because the load-bearing contract-term and renewal language sits in the WV Board of Education legislative rule (126 CSR 202 / Policy 8200) rather than in a transportation-specific codified statute, and the West Virginia Code itself is silent on a maximum contract term for student-transportation services contracts. The 'two successive one year periods' renewal cap in Policy 8200 is generally applicable to LEA service contracts and is not articulated specifically for transportation contracts in any located transportation-specific authority. District-Level Interpretation Variance remains 'Unknown' as a documented negative — Policy 8200 structurally permits stricter local supplementation, but no statewide WVDE bulletin, state-auditor report, AG opinion, or West Virginia School Boards Association guidance affirmatively documenting either uniform application or actual interpretive variance for the five-field framework was located.
verificationNotes.conflictChecks: Cross-statute consistency checked: §18-5-13 (county-board transportation authority) vs. §5-22-1 (construction-only competitive bidding for state and subdivisions including county BOEs) vs. §5A-3-10 (state-spending-unit Purchasing Division procurement) vs. §5A-3-11 (state contracts for school-bus equipment) — confirmed no conflict because each operates on a different subject-matter and the only generally-applicable rule for county-board student-transportation services contracts is the BOE-promulgated Policy 8200. Cross-rule consistency checked: Policy 8200 §3.2.5.c (renewal cap) vs. §19.1.1 (separate school-bus award framework via §5A-3-11) — no conflict, because §19.1.1 governs purchase of bus equipment, not transportation services. Cross-policy consistency checked: Policy 8200 vs. Policy 4336 §17.5 (contract operators) — no conflict, because §17.5 sets only individual operator qualifications, not contract term/renewal/procurement.

notesSpecialProvisions: West Virginia's framework for student-transportation contracts is governed almost entirely by W. Va. Board of Education legislative rule Policy 8200 (W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202), implemented under the county-board transportation authority of W. Va. Code §18-5-13. Several discretionary mechanisms within Policy 8200 affect the practical operation of the five fields: small-purchase / dollar-threshold treatment is structured around §7.11's tiered bid thresholds (verbal quotes at $5,000, written bids at $10,000, advertised bids at $25,000, sealed advertised bids at $50,000), with LEAs free to lower the thresholds further by local policy; sole-source / direct-purchase authority is recognized under §3.2.5.a(6) and the §8.1 alternative-procurement procedures (which also cover situations where no bidders respond); cooperative-purchasing / piggyback authority is recognized for State open-ended contracts (§§3.2.5.a.c, 8.1.4), GSA schedules (§8.1.5), RESA and inter-county cooperatives (§8.1.7), and national governmental purchasing cooperatives such as AEPA, U.S. Communities, and TCPN (§8.1.8); §9 exempts an enumerated list of services from competitive bid (including the annual audit and certain professional services). The WV Code provides a separate, equipment-only framework for state contracts on school buses through §5A-3-11, which is administered through Policy 8200 §19.1.1 and which does not reach transportation services contracts. Charter schools are addressed in W. Va. Code §18-5G-5, which adjusts transportation funding allocations but does not establish a different procurement framework for charter-school transportation contracts. Special-education student transportation is required under §18-20-2 and is operationalized through Policy 2419 (126 CSR 16) and Policy 4336 (126 CSR 92) — including IEP/§504-specified bus modifications — but no separate procurement, term, or renewal rule applies; Policy 4336 §17.5's 'contract operators' framework governs individual operator qualifications (background checks, training, $1,000,000 auto-liability minimum) in limited-service areas rather than the LEA's procurement of a transportation services contract. No state-agency approval is required to renew a county-board transportation contract; renewals require only mutual written consent of the LEA and vendor, a written letter of justification on file, and compliance with the two-successive-one-year cap. Policy 8200 expressly contemplates that LEAs will adopt local supplementing policies that may make purchasing requirements more restrictive but may not relax the state-board floor.
exceptionsUnresolved: Two items remain documented-open rather than definitively answered. (1) Whether a transportation-specific maximum contract term exists in any West Virginia codified statute — Stage 1 located none, and Stage 2 confirmed the negative search path against W. Va. Code Chapters 18 (Education), 18A (School Personnel), 5A-3 (Purchasing Division), 5-22 (Public Construction Contracts), and 17C-14 (School Bus Rules), and against the 2025 and 2026 W. Va. Legislature Regular Session bill lists; the answer remains 'No statutory maximum found' on a verified negative basis. (2) District-Level Interpretation Variance — Stage 1 recorded 'Unknown' as a documented negative, and Stage 2 sustained that label. Disconfirming searches against state-auditor reports, AG opinions, WVDE bulletins, and West Virginia School Boards Association guidance did not surface either uniform application or material interpretive variance for the five-field framework as applied to student-transportation services contracts. Targeted district-portal checks (Kanawha, Berkeley-region, and Cabell-region purchasing/policy materials) did not locate a published district policy that materially diverges from Policy 8200 in a way that would change the five-field answer for that district; that absence is itself a documented negative, not affirmative evidence of uniformity. All other fields are resolved as 'confirmed' against the official text of W. Va. Code §18-5-13 and W. Va. C.S.R. §126-202 (Policy 8200).
